title: trifolium dasyphyllum f. attenuatum
intro: Trifolium dasyphyllum f. attenuatum, also known as a variant of the mountain clover, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and other compounds. The health benefits of Trifolium dasyphyllum f. attenuatum include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
